response.justification: The candidate is a capable, experienced full-stack generalist, but the profile is a poor match for this role. The position demands strong C for UNIX, hands-on Linux kernel source modification, embedded networking-security expertise (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3 switching), and native-level Japanese. The candidate's C/C++ exposure is brief and dated (a ~6-month stint at National Instruments and a university thesis), with no embedded-firmware, network-switch, or security-protocol experience, and no indication of Japanese proficiency. The core work history centers on TypeScript/Node, DLang, and web/GIS platforms.
strengths: Broad, long software career spanning web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded work
Some C/C++ background (National Instruments LabWindows/CVI, C++ thesis project)
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, Linux/Docker, dev-ops tooling
concerns: No embedded networking-security experience (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3, switch chips)
No Linux kernel source-modification work; C skills are minimal and dated
No evidence of native-level Japanese proficiency (only EN/RO fluent, basic DE)

key_factors.factor: Lack of C/UNIX and Linux kernel depth
